mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and increases the present value of equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the easing is not overwhelmed by recession signals.
evidence_refs: fact:Before 2008-01-15 the Federal Reserve had begun cutting rates from the 2007 peak
failure_condition: Reject if falling 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by non-positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply over 3 months, household income risk and recession probability increase, causing equity investors to mark down expected earnings and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change crosses above +0.3 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:The December 2007 unemployment rate release was available before 2008-01-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of cases or if triggered returns are not lower than unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A large decline in HOUST signals weakening residential investment, tighter credit creation, and lower future cyclical earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its trailing 12-month average by more than 15%
evidence_refs: fact:Housing activity had weakened materially before 2008-01-15
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-triggered observations do not have lower 60-day forward S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: FEDFUNDS cuts combined with a rising UNRATE indicate policy easing in response to recession risk rather than benign discount-rate relief, so earnings downgrades dominate and S&P 500 returns are negative over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change < -0.25 percentage points and UNRATE 3-month change > +0.2 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:By 2008-01-15 rate cuts coincided with weakening labor-market data
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS-down and UNRATE-up state has higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than FEDFUNDS-down alone, or if its negative-return hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in INDPRO signals expanding output and improving operating leverage for listed firms, which supports earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month INDPRO growth fails to predict higher 20-day S&P 500 returns with rolling 3-year Rank IC above 0.03, or if the top-minus-bottom signal spread is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth falls below CPIAUCSL inflation, real retail spending contracts, implying weaker consumption-led revenue growth and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month RSAFS growth crosses below 3-month CPIAUCSL growth
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS-minus-CPIAUCSL real-spending trigger is not followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in PAYEMS indicates continued job creation and supports aggregate wage income, consumption, and corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days after release.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS-positive crossings have non-positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if their return distribution is not statistically above PAYEMS-negative states at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL inflation combined with declining FEDFUNDS signals falling real policy rates and inflation pressure, which raises risk premia and reduces equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth crosses above 3% while FEDFUNDS 3-month change is negative
failure_condition: Reject if this CPIAUCSL-up and FEDFUNDS-down interaction has positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns or if it does not underperform periods with falling CPIAUCSL growth.
